title: PassbackAI interactive components — there are only two: questionnaire and prioritize
description: JSON Schema for the interactive components PassbackAI renders inside a pasted Markdown document. There are EXACTLY TWO renderable block types, each a bare JSON object inside its own fenced block: ```questionnaire and ```prioritize. No other fence tag renders (```multiple-choice, ```open-question, ```quiz, ```poll, etc. are NOT block types — they show as plain code). Question types (single-select, multi-select via `multi:true`, free-text via empty `options`) are MODES inside one questionnaire block, never separate blocks. A payload validates against exactly one component. Human spec: https://passbackai.com/ask ; raw Markdown: https://passbackai.com/ask.md — this file is kept in sync with them in the same commit.
$comment: Spec revision r6 (2026-06-21). The fence contract: only `questionnaire` and `prioritize` render; everything else is a plain code block. This JSON Schema validates the JSON INSIDE a fence — it cannot enforce the fence tag itself, so the renderer contract is documented in /ask.md §2. Wire `version` is the string "1" for every component; `skill_version` is unrelated authoring-build metadata. Guards not expressible here, noted in `description` fields: (a) questionnaire question ids should be unique — duplicates collide answer state but are not rejected; (b) prioritize item ids MUST be unique — a duplicate rejects the block; (c) `recommended` and `routing.return_prompt` cross-field rules. Validate structure here, then consult /ask.md for the full guard list and the fence contract.

routing.description: Include only when the document is sent to someone else. `return_prompt` should contain the literal value of `from` so the renderer can bold the name.

questionnaire.title: Questionnaire (verb: choose)
questionnaire.description: A list of questions; each is a single/multi-select or a pure free-text field. Embedded as a ```questionnaire fence, or pasted as a bare JSON object on its own.

version.description: The wire schema version. Always the string "1" — the number 1 is rejected.

skill_version.description: Authoring-skill build tag (currently "1.5"). Optional metadata; does not gate rendering.

id.description: Should be unique within the questionnaire; duplicates are not rejected but collide answer state.

question.description: The prompt. The key must be `question` — never `q`/`text`/`prompt`.

options.description: Plain strings only — an option object is rejected. Do not include an "Other" entry; the renderer adds a free-text row automatically. May be [] for a pure free-text question, which then requires `open_field`.

multi.description: true = checkboxes (multi-select). Must be a boolean — "true"/1 are rejected.

recommended.description: Mark-only nudge: an option label (string), or an array of labels when `multi` is true. Must exactly match an existing option label; a non-matching value is silently ignored. Never pre-selects and never echoed into the answers.

open_field.description: Overrides the default "Other:" label. Required when `options` is [].

allow_note.description: Always omit — every option-bearing question always shows a note field.

allOf.$comment: A question with no options is pure free-text and must ship an explicit open_field.

prioritize.title: Prioritize (verb: order)
prioritize.description: A short list the user ranks. Embedded only inside a ```prioritize fence (no bare-JSON shortcut). The array order is the suggested starting order.

items.description: Item ids MUST be unique across the array — a duplicate id rejects the whole block. (JSON Schema cannot express per-property uniqueness; enforce it on your side.)
